lines.gurmukhi: ਕਬੀਰ ਮਾਨਸ ਜਨਮੁ ਦੁਲੰਭੁ ਹੈ ਹੋਇ ਨ ਬਾਰੈ ਬਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: kabeer maanas janam dulanbh hai hoe na baarai baar |
lines.english: Kabeer, it is so difficult to obtain this human body; it does not just come over and over again.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਉ ਬਨ ਫਲ ਪਾਕੇ ਭੁਇ ਗਿਰਹਿ ਬਹੁਰਿ ਨ ਲਾਗਹਿ ਡਾਰ ॥੩੦॥
lines.translit: jiau ban fal paake bhue gireh bahur na laageh ddaar |30|
lines.english: It is like the ripe fruit on the tree; when it falls to the ground, it cannot be re-attached to the branch. ||30||

lines.gurmukhi: ਐਸੇ ਮਰਨੇ ਜੋ ਮਰੈ ਬਹੁਰਿ ਨ ਮਰਨਾ ਹੋਇ ॥੨੯॥
lines.translit: aise marane jo marai bahur na maranaa hoe |29|
lines.english: Let those who die, die such a death, that they shall never have to die again. ||29||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਬੀਰਾ ਤੁਹੀ ਕਬੀਰੁ ਤੂ ਤੇਰੋ ਨਾਉ ਕਬੀਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: kabeeraa tuhee kabeer too tero naau kabeer |
lines.english: Kabeer, you are Kabeer; your name means great.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਬੀਰ ਝੰਖੁ ਨ ਝੰਖੀਐ ਤੁਮਰੋ ਕਹਿਓ ਨ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: kabeer jhankh na jhankheeai tumaro kahio na hoe |
lines.english: Kabeer, do not struggle in stubborn pride; nothing happens just because you say so.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮ ਰਤਨੁ ਤਬ ਪਾਈਐ ਜਉ ਪਹਿਲੇ ਤਜਹਿ ਸਰੀਰੁ ॥੩੧॥
lines.translit: raam ratan tab paaeeai jau pahile tajeh sareer |31|
lines.english: O Lord, You are Kabeer. The Jewel of the Lord is obtained, when the mortal first gives up his body. ||31||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਬੀਰ ਕਸਉਟੀ ਰਾਮ ਕੀ ਝੂਠਾ ਟਿਕੈ ਨ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: kabeer ksauttee raam kee jhootthaa ttikai na koe |
lines.english: Kabeer, no one who is false can withstand the Touchstone of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਮ ਕਰੀਮ ਜੁ ਕਰਿ ਰਹੇ ਮੇਟਿ ਨ ਸਾਕੈ ਕੋਇ ॥੩੨॥
lines.translit: karam kareem ju kar rahe mett na saakai koe |32|
lines.english: No one can erase the actions of the Merciful Lord. ||32||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮ ਕਸਉਟੀ ਸੋ ਸਹੈ ਜੋ ਮਰਿ ਜੀਵਾ ਹੋਇ ॥੩੩॥
lines.translit: raam ksauttee so sahai jo mar jeevaa hoe |33|
lines.english: He alone can pass the test of the Lord's Touchstone, who remains dead while yet alive. ||33||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਬੀਰ ਊਜਲ ਪਹਿਰਹਿ ਕਾਪਰੇ ਪਾਨ ਸੁਪਾਰੀ ਖਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: kabeer aoojal pahireh kaapare paan supaaree khaeh |
lines.english: Kabeer, some wear gaudy robes, and chew betel leaves and betel nuts.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰੂਏ ਹਰੂਏ ਤਿਰਿ ਗਏ ਡੂਬੇ ਜਿਨ ਸਿਰ ਭਾਰ ॥੩੫॥
lines.translit: harooe harooe tir ge ddoobe jin sir bhaar |35|
lines.english: Those who are light get across, while those who carry the weight of their sins on their heads are drowned. ||35||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਬੀਰ ਬੇੜਾ ਜਰਜਰਾ ਫੂਟੇ ਛੇਂਕ ਹਜਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: kabeer berraa jarajaraa footte chhenk hajaar |
lines.english: Kabeer, the boat is old, and it has thousands of holes.

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਕਸ ਹਰਿ ਕੇ ਨਾਮ ਬਿਨੁ ਬਾਧੇ ਜਮ ਪੁਰਿ ਜਾਂਹਿ ॥੩੪॥
lines.translit: ekas har ke naam bin baadhe jam pur jaanhi |34|
lines.english: Without the Name of the One Lord, they are bound and gagged and taken to the City of Death. ||34||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਬੀਰ ਹਾਡ ਜਰੇ ਜਿਉ ਲਾਕਰੀ ਕੇਸ ਜਰੇ ਜਿਉ ਘਾਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: kabeer haadd jare jiau laakaree kes jare jiau ghaas |
lines.english: Kabeer, the bones burn like wood, and the hair burns like straw.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹੁ ਜਗੁ ਜਰਤਾ ਦੇਖਿ ਕੈ ਭਇਓ ਕਬੀਰੁ ਉਦਾਸੁ ॥੩੬॥
lines.translit: eihu jag jarataa dekh kai bheo kabeer udaas |36|
lines.english: Seeing the world burning like this, Kabeer has become sad. ||36||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੈਵਰ ਊਪਰਿ ਛਤ੍ਰ ਤਰ ਤੇ ਫੁਨਿ ਧਰਨੀ ਗਾਡ ॥੩੭॥
lines.translit: haivar aoopar chhatr tar te fun dharanee gaadd |37|
lines.english: Those who were on their horses and under their canopies, were eventually buried under the ground. ||37||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਬੀਰ ਗਰਬੁ ਨ ਕੀਜੀਐ ਚਾਮ ਲਪੇਟੇ ਹਾਡ ॥
lines.translit: kabeer garab na keejeeai chaam lapette haadd |
lines.english: Kabeer, do not be so proud of your bones wrapped up in skin.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਜੁ ਕਾਲੑਿ ਭੁਇ ਲੇਟਣਾ ਊਪਰਿ ਜਾਮੈ ਘਾਸੁ ॥੩੮॥
lines.translit: aaj kaala bhue lettanaa aoopar jaamai ghaas |38|
lines.english: Today or tomorrow, you shall lie beneath the ground, and the grass shall grow above you. ||38||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਬੀਰ ਗਰਬੁ ਨ ਕੀਜੀਐ ਊਚਾ ਦੇਖਿ ਅਵਾਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: kabeer garab na keejeeai aoochaa dekh avaas |
lines.english: Kabeer, do not be so proud of your tall mansions.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਬੀਰ ਗਰਬੁ ਨ ਕੀਜੀਐ ਰੰਕੁ ਨ ਹਸੀਐ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: kabeer garab na keejeeai rank na haseeai koe |
lines.english: Kabeer, do not be so proud, and do not laugh at the poor.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਜਹੁ ਸੁ ਨਾਉ ਸਮੁੰਦ੍ਰ ਮਹਿ ਕਿਆ ਜਾਨਉ ਕਿਆ ਹੋਇ ॥੩੯॥
lines.translit: ajahu su naau samundr meh kiaa jaanau kiaa hoe |39|
lines.english: Your boat is still out at sea; who knows what will happen? ||39||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਬੀਰ ਗਰਬੁ ਨ ਕੀਜੀਐ ਦੇਹੀ ਦੇਖਿ ਸੁਰੰਗ ॥
lines.translit: kabeer garab na keejeeai dehee dekh surang |
lines.english: Kabeer, do not be so proud, looking at your beautiful body.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਜੁ ਕਾਲੑਿ ਤਜਿ ਜਾਹੁਗੇ ਜਿਉ ਕਾਂਚੁਰੀ ਭੁਯੰਗ ॥੪੦॥
lines.translit: aaj kaala taj jaahuge jiau kaanchuree bhuyang |40|
lines.english: Today or tomorrow, you will have to leave it behind, like the snake shedding its skin. ||40||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਿਰਿ ਪਾਛੈ ਪਛੁਤਾਹੁਗੇ ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਜਾਹਿੰਗੇ ਛੂਟਿ ॥੪੧॥
lines.translit: fir paachhai pachhutaahuge praan jaahinge chhoott |41|
lines.english: Otherwise, in the world hereafter, you will regret and repent, when the breath of life leaves the body. ||41||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਬੀਰ ਲੂਟਨਾ ਹੈ ਤ ਲੂਟਿ ਲੈ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਹੈ ਲੂਟਿ ॥
lines.translit: kabeer loottanaa hai ta loott lai raam naam hai loott |
lines.english: Kabeer, if you must rob and plunder, then plunder the plunder of the Lord's Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਬੀਰ ਐਸਾ ਕੋਈ ਨ ਜਨਮਿਓ ਅਪਨੈ ਘਰਿ ਲਾਵੈ ਆਗਿ ॥
lines.translit: kabeer aisaa koee na janamio apanai ghar laavai aag |
lines.english: Kabeer, there is no one born, who burns his own home,

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਂਚਉ ਲਰਿਕਾ ਜਾਰਿ ਕੈ ਰਹੈ ਰਾਮ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਗਿ ॥੪੨॥
lines.translit: paanchau larikaa jaar kai rahai raam liv laag |42|
lines.english: and burning his five sons, remains lovingly attuned to the Lord. ||42||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੋ ਹੈ ਲਰਿਕਾ ਬੇਚਈ ਲਰਿਕੀ ਬੇਚੈ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: ko hai larikaa bechee larikee bechai koe |
lines.english: Kabeer, how rare are those who sell their son and sell their daughter

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਝਾ ਕਰੈ ਕਬੀਰ ਸਿਉ ਹਰਿ ਸੰਗਿ ਬਨਜੁ ਕਰੇਇ ॥੪੩॥
lines.translit: saajhaa karai kabeer siau har sang banaj karee |43|
lines.english: and, entering into partnership with Kabeer, deal with the Lord. ||43||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਬੀਰ ਇਹ ਚੇਤਾਵਨੀ ਮਤ ਸਹਸਾ ਰਹਿ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: kabeer ih chetaavanee mat sahasaa reh jaae |
lines.english: Kabeer, let me remind you of this. Do not be skeptical or cynical.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਛੈ ਭੋਗ ਜੁ ਭੋਗਵੇ ਤਿਨ ਕੋ ਗੁੜੁ ਲੈ ਖਾਹਿ ॥੪੪॥
lines.translit: paachhai bhog ju bhogave tin ko gurr lai khaeh |44|
lines.english: Those pleasures which you enjoyed so much in the past - now you must eat their fruits. ||44||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਬੀਰ ਮੈ ਜਾਨਿਓ ਪੜਿਬੋ ਭਲੋ ਪੜਿਬੇ ਸਿਉ ਭਲ ਜੋਗੁ ॥
lines.translit: kabeer mai jaanio parribo bhalo parribe siau bhal jog |
lines.english: Kabeer, at first, I thought learning was good; then I thought Yoga was better.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਗਤਿ ਨ ਛਾਡਉ ਰਾਮ ਕੀ ਭਾਵੈ ਨਿੰਦਉ ਲੋਗੁ ॥੪੫॥
lines.translit: bhagat na chhaaddau raam kee bhaavai nindau log |45|
lines.english: I shall never abandon devotional worship of the Lord, even though people may slander me. ||45||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਬੀਰ ਲੋਗੁ ਕਿ ਨਿੰਦੈ ਬਪੁੜਾ ਜਿਹ ਮਨਿ ਨਾਹੀ ਗਿਆਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: kabeer log ki nindai bapurraa jih man naahee giaan |
lines.english: Kabeer, how can the wretched people slander me? They have no wisdom or intelligence.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮ ਕਬੀਰਾ ਰਵਿ ਰਹੇ ਅਵਰ ਤਜੇ ਸਭ ਕਾਮ ॥੪੬॥
lines.translit: raam kabeeraa rav rahe avar taje sabh kaam |46|
lines.english: Kabeer continues to dwell upon the Lord's Name; I have abandoned all other affairs. ||46||

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਿੰਥਾ ਜਲਿ ਕੋਇਲਾ ਭਈ ਤਾਗੇ ਆਂਚ ਨ ਲਾਗ ॥੪੭॥
lines.translit: khinthaa jal koeilaa bhee taage aanch na laag |47|
lines.english: The cloth of the body has been burnt and reduced to charcoal, but the fire did not touch the thread of the soul. ||47||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਬੀਰ ਪਰਦੇਸੀ ਕੈ ਘਾਘਰੈ ਚਹੁ ਦਿਸਿ ਲਾਗੀ ਆਗਿ ॥
lines.translit: kabeer paradesee kai ghaagharai chahu dis laagee aag |
lines.english: Kabeer, the robe of the stranger-soul has caught fire on all four sides.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋਗੀ ਬਪੁੜਾ ਖੇਲਿਓ ਆਸਨਿ ਰਹੀ ਬਿਭੂਤਿ ॥੪੮॥
lines.translit: jogee bapurraa khelio aasan rahee bibhoot |48|
lines.english: The poor Yogi has played out his game; only ashes remain on his seat. ||48||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਬੀਰ ਖਿੰਥਾ ਜਲਿ ਕੋਇਲਾ ਭਈ ਖਾਪਰੁ ਫੂਟ ਮਫੂਟ ॥
lines.translit: kabeer khinthaa jal koeilaa bhee khaapar foott mafoott |
lines.english: Kabeer, the cloth has been burnt and reduced to charcoal, and the begging bowl is shattered into pieces.
